steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts Barragan's early work (historically inspired houses for Emiliano Robles León) with his later designs that incorporated modernism after his travels. The decisive clue is the phrase “Barragan began incorporating principles derived from functionalism and modernism in his work,” linked directly to his international travels in the 1930s. This signals a shift in aesthetic inspired by external influences.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice D directly ties the architectural shift to Barragan’s time abroad, aligning with the cause-effect structure in the passage. A misrepresents the scope by labeling two projects as “paragons” rather than showing a career shift; B invents a contrast not supported in the text; C incorrectly states a reversal of style. That matches choice D — D is correct.
